summata 0.9.3
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Convert Model to Data Table

Source: R/m2dt.R
m2dt.Rd

Extracts coefficients, confidence intervals, and comprehensive model statistics from fitted regression models and converts them to a standardized data.table format suitable for further analysis or publication. This is a core utility function used internally by other fullfit functions.

m2dt(
  data,
  model,
  conf_level = 0.95,
  keep_qc_stats = TRUE,
  include_intercept = TRUE,
  terms_to_exclude = NULL,
  reference_rows = TRUE,
  reference_label = "reference",
  skip_counts = FALSE
)

Arguments

data

A data.frame or data.table containing the dataset used to fit the model. Required for computing group-level sample sizes and event counts.

model

A fitted model object. Supported classes include:

  • glm - Generalized linear models (logistic, Poisson, etc.)

  • lm - Linear models

  • coxph - Cox proportional hazards models

  • clogit - Conditional logistic regression

  • coxme - Mixed effects Cox models

  • lmerMod - Linear mixed effects models

  • glmerMod - Generalized linear mixed effects models

conf_level

Numeric confidence level for confidence intervals. Must be between 0 and 1. Default is 0.95 (95% CI).

keep_qc_stats

Logical. If TRUE, includes model quality statistics such as AIC, BIC, R-squared, concordance, and model fit tests. These appear as additional columns in the output. Default is TRUE.

include_intercept

Logical. If TRUE, includes the model intercept in output. If FALSE, removes the intercept row from results. Useful for creating cleaner presentation tables. Default is TRUE.

terms_to_exclude

Character vector of term names to exclude from output. Useful for removing specific unwanted parameters (e.g., nuisance variables, spline terms). Default is NULL. Note: If include_intercept = FALSE, "(Intercept)" is automatically added to this list.

reference_rows

Logical. If TRUE, adds rows for reference categories of factor variables with appropriate labels and baseline values (OR/HR = 1, Coefficient = 0). This makes tables more complete and easier to interpret. Default is TRUE.

reference_label

Character string used to label reference category rows in the output. Appears in the reference column. Default is "reference".

skip_counts

Logical. If TRUE, skips computation of group-level sample sizes and event counts (faster but less informative). Default is FALSE.

Value

A data.table containing extracted model information with the following standard columns:

model_scope

Character. Either "Univariable" (unadjusted model with single predictor) or "Multivariable" (adjusted model with multiple predictors)

model_type

Character. Type of regression (e.g., "Logistic", "Linear", "Cox PH", "Poisson")

variable

Character. Variable name (for factor variables, the base variable name without the level)

group

Character. Group/level name for factor variables; empty string for continuous variables

n

Integer. Total sample size used in the model

n_group

Integer. Sample size for this specific variable level (factor variables only)

events

Integer. Total number of events in the model (for survival and logistic models)

events_group

Integer. Number of events for this specific variable level (for survival and logistic models with factor variables)

coefficient

Numeric. Raw regression coefficient (log odds, log hazard, etc.)

se

Numeric. Standard error of the coefficient

OR/HR/RR/Coefficient

Numeric. Effect estimate - column name depends on model type:

  • OR for logistic regression (odds ratio)

  • HR for Cox models (hazard ratio)

  • RR for Poisson regression (rate/risk ratio)

  • Coefficient for linear models or other GLMs

ci_lower

Numeric. Lower bound of confidence interval for effect estimate

ci_upper

Numeric. Upper bound of confidence interval for effect estimate

statistic

Numeric. Test statistic (z-value for GLM/Cox, t-value for LM)

p_value

Numeric. P-value for coefficient test

sig

Character. Significance markers: "***" (p<0.001), "**" (p<0.01), "*" (p<0.05), "." (p<0.10), "" (p≥0.10)

sig_binary

Logical. Binary indicator: TRUE if p<0.05, FALSE otherwise

reference

Character. Contains reference_label for reference category rows when reference_rows = TRUE, empty string otherwise

Details

This function is the core extraction utility used by fit and other functions. It handles the complexities of different model classes and provides a consistent output format suitable for tables and forest plots.

Model Type Detection: The function automatically detects model type and applies appropriate:

  • Effect measure naming (OR, HR, RR, Coefficient)

  • Confidence interval calculation method

  • Event counting for binary/survival outcomes

Mixed Effects Models: For lme4 models (glmer, lmer), the function extracts fixed effects only. Random effects structure is not included in the output table.

See also

fit for the main regression interface, glmforest, coxforest, lmforest for forest plot visualization

Examples

# Load example data
data(clintrial)

# Example 1: Extract from logistic regression
glm_model <- glm(os_status ~ age + sex + treatment, 
                 data = clintrial, family = binomial)

glm_result <- m2dt(clintrial, glm_model)
glm_result
#>      model_scope model_type             term     n events coefficient
#>           <char>     <char>           <char> <int>  <num>       <num>
#> 1: Multivariable   Logistic      (Intercept)   850    628 -1.15943597
#> 2: Multivariable   Logistic              age   850    628  0.04113579
#> 3: Multivariable   Logistic        sexFemale   450    324  0.00000000
#> 4: Multivariable   Logistic          sexMale   400    304  0.18742900
#> 5: Multivariable   Logistic treatmentControl   196    154  0.00000000
#> 6: Multivariable   Logistic  treatmentDrug A   292    183 -0.83712583
#> 7: Multivariable   Logistic  treatmentDrug B   362    291  0.06163894
#>            se        coef  coef_lower  coef_upper  exp_coef exp_lower exp_upper
#>         <num>       <num>       <num>       <num>     <num>     <num>     <num>
#> 1: 0.44641426 -1.15943597 -2.03439183 -0.28448010 0.3136630 0.1307600 0.7524053
#> 2: 0.00712909  0.04113579  0.02716303  0.05510855 1.0419936 1.0275353 1.0566553
#> 3:         NA  0.00000000          NA          NA 1.0000000        NA        NA
#> 4: 0.16371242  0.18742900 -0.13344145  0.50829945 1.2061446 0.8750787 1.6624617
#> 5:         NA  0.00000000          NA          NA 1.0000000        NA        NA
#> 6: 0.21756836 -0.83712583 -1.26355198 -0.41069969 0.4329531 0.2826483 0.6631861
#> 7: 0.22320295  0.06163894 -0.37583081  0.49910869 1.0635783 0.6867185 1.6472524
#>           OR  ci_lower  ci_upper  statistic      p_value      AIC      BIC
#>        <num>     <num>     <num>      <num>        <num>    <num>    <num>
#> 1: 0.3136630 0.1307600 0.7524053 -2.5972198 9.398175e-03 921.2366 944.9628
#> 2: 1.0419936 1.0275353 1.0566553  5.7701312 7.920986e-09 921.2366 944.9628
#> 3: 1.0000000        NA        NA         NA           NA 921.2366 944.9628
#> 4: 1.2061446 0.8750787 1.6624617  1.1448673 2.522641e-01 921.2366 944.9628
#> 5: 1.0000000        NA        NA         NA           NA 921.2366 944.9628
#> 6: 0.4329531 0.2826483 0.6631861 -3.8476451 1.192586e-04 921.2366 944.9628
#> 7: 1.0635783 0.6867185 1.6472524  0.2761565 7.824279e-01 921.2366 944.9628
#>    deviance null_deviance df_residual c_statistic hoslem_chi2   hoslem_p
#>       <num>         <num>       <int>       <num>       <num>      <num>
#> 1: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 2: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 3: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 4: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 5: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 6: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 7: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#>       variable   group n_group events_group reference    sig sig_binary
#>         <char>  <char>   <num>        <num>    <char> <char>     <lgcl>
#> 1: (Intercept)              NA           NA               **       TRUE
#> 2:         age              NA           NA              ***       TRUE
#> 3:         sex  Female     450          324 reference             FALSE
#> 4:         sex    Male     400          304                       FALSE
#> 5:   treatment Control     196          154 reference             FALSE
#> 6:   treatment  Drug A     292          183              ***       TRUE
#> 7:   treatment  Drug B     362          291                       FALSE

# Example 2: Extract from linear model
lm_model <- lm(los_days ~ age + sex + surgery, data = clintrial)

lm_result <- m2dt(clintrial, lm_model)
lm_result
#>      model_scope model_type        term     n events coefficient         se
#>           <char>     <char>      <char> <int>  <int>       <num>      <num>
#> 1: Multivariable     Linear (Intercept)   830     NA  10.1480443 0.87333008
#> 2: Multivariable     Linear         age   830     NA   0.1487493 0.01344942
#> 3: Multivariable     Linear   sexFemale   450     NA   0.0000000         NA
#> 4: Multivariable     Linear     sexMale   400     NA   0.9071448 0.31347883
#> 5: Multivariable     Linear   surgeryNo   480     NA   0.0000000         NA
#> 6: Multivariable     Linear  surgeryYes   370     NA   1.3070747 0.32101589
#>          coef coef_lower coef_upper   exp_coef exp_lower  exp_upper Coefficient
#>         <num>      <num>      <num>      <num>     <num>      <num>       <num>
#> 1: 10.1480443  8.4363488 11.8597399 10.1480443 8.4363488 11.8597399  10.1480443
#> 2:  0.1487493  0.1223889  0.1751097  0.1487493 0.1223889  0.1751097   0.1487493
#> 3:  0.0000000         NA         NA  1.0000000        NA         NA   0.9071448
#> 4:  0.9071448  0.2927376  1.5215520  0.9071448 0.2927376  1.5215520   0.9071448
#> 5:  0.0000000         NA         NA  1.0000000        NA         NA   1.3070747
#> 6:  1.3070747  0.6778951  1.9362543  1.3070747 0.6778951  1.9362543   1.3070747
#>     ci_lower   ci_upper statistic      p_value        R2    adj_R2      AIC
#>        <num>      <num>     <num>        <num>     <num>     <num>    <num>
#> 1: 8.4363488 11.8597399 11.619941 5.128976e-29 0.1392244 0.1360981 4859.439
#> 2: 0.1223889  0.1751097 11.059904 1.302192e-26 0.1392244 0.1360981 4859.439
#> 3: 0.2927376  1.5215520        NA           NA 0.1392244 0.1360981 4859.439
#> 4: 0.2927376  1.5215520  2.893799 3.906286e-03 0.1392244 0.1360981 4859.439
#> 5: 0.6778951  1.9362543        NA           NA 0.1392244 0.1360981 4859.439
#> 6: 0.6778951  1.9362543  4.071682 5.116365e-05 0.1392244 0.1360981 4859.439
#>         BIC    sigma df_residual    variable  group n_group events_group
#>       <num>    <num>       <int>      <char> <char>   <num>        <num>
#> 1: 4883.046 4.503368         826 (Intercept)             NA           NA
#> 2: 4883.046 4.503368         826         age             NA           NA
#> 3: 4883.046 4.503368         826         sex Female     450           NA
#> 4: 4883.046 4.503368         826         sex   Male     400           NA
#> 5: 4883.046 4.503368         826     surgery     No     480           NA
#> 6: 4883.046 4.503368         826     surgery    Yes     370           NA
#>    reference    sig sig_binary
#>       <char> <char>     <lgcl>
#> 1:              ***       TRUE
#> 2:              ***       TRUE
#> 3: reference             FALSE
#> 4:               **       TRUE
#> 5: reference             FALSE
#> 6:              ***       TRUE

# Example 3: Cox proportional hazards model
library(survival)
cox_model <- coxph(Surv(os_months, os_status) ~ age + sex + stage,
                   data = clintrial)

cox_result <- m2dt(clintrial, cox_model)
cox_result
#>      model_scope model_type      term     n events coefficient          se
#>           <char>     <char>    <char> <int>  <num>       <num>       <num>
#> 1: Multivariable     Cox PH       age   847    626  0.02710701 0.003509385
#> 2: Multivariable     Cox PH sexFemale   450    324  0.00000000          NA
#> 3: Multivariable     Cox PH   sexMale   400    304  0.21464566 0.080517307
#> 4: Multivariable     Cox PH    stageI   211    131  0.00000000          NA
#> 5: Multivariable     Cox PH   stageII   264    184  0.25408307 0.114436526
#> 6: Multivariable     Cox PH  stageIII   240    194  0.62156299 0.113842601
#> 7: Multivariable     Cox PH   stageIV   132    117  1.11169965 0.129085573
#>          coef coef_lower coef_upper exp_coef exp_lower exp_upper       HR
#>         <num>      <num>      <num>    <num>     <num>     <num>    <num>
#> 1: 0.02710701 0.02022874 0.03398527 1.027478  1.020435  1.034569 1.027478
#> 2: 0.00000000         NA         NA 1.000000        NA        NA 1.000000
#> 3: 0.21464566 0.05683464 0.37245668 1.239423  1.058481  1.451296 1.239423
#> 4: 0.00000000         NA         NA 1.000000        NA        NA 1.000000
#> 5: 0.25408307 0.02979160 0.47837454 1.289279  1.030240  1.613450 1.289279
#> 6: 0.62156299 0.39843559 0.84469038 1.861836  1.489493  2.327257 1.861836
#> 7: 1.11169965 0.85869658 1.36470273 3.039520  2.360083  3.914559 3.039520
#>    ci_lower ci_upper statistic      p_value concordance concordance_se
#>       <num>    <num>     <num>        <num>       <num>          <num>
#> 1: 1.020435 1.034569  7.724146 1.126058e-14   0.6468413     0.01122331
#> 2:       NA       NA        NA           NA   0.6468413     0.01122331
#> 3: 1.058481 1.451296  2.665833 7.679794e-03   0.6468413     0.01122331
#> 4:       NA       NA        NA           NA   0.6468413     0.01122331
#> 5: 1.030240 1.613450  2.220297 2.639861e-02   0.6468413     0.01122331
#> 6: 1.489493 2.327257  5.459845 4.765497e-08   0.6468413     0.01122331
#> 7: 2.360083 3.914559  8.612114 7.172557e-18   0.6468413     0.01122331
#>          rsq   rsq_max likelihood_ratio_test likelihood_ratio_df
#>        <num>     <num>                 <num>               <num>
#> 1: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#> 2: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#> 3: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#> 4: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#> 5: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#> 6: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#> 7: 0.1518056 0.9998915              139.4547                   5
#>    likelihood_ratio_p wald_test wald_df       wald_p score_test score_df
#>                 <num>     <num>   <num>        <num>      <num>    <num>
#> 1:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#> 2:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#> 3:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#> 4:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#> 5:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#> 6:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#> 7:       2.336561e-28    143.13       5 3.872633e-29   147.2262        5
#>         score_p variable  group n_group events_group reference    sig
#>           <num>   <char> <char>   <num>        <num>    <char> <char>
#> 1: 5.198291e-30      age             NA           NA              ***
#> 2: 5.198291e-30      sex Female     450          324 reference       
#> 3: 5.198291e-30      sex   Male     400          304               **
#> 4: 5.198291e-30    stage      I     211          131 reference       
#> 5: 5.198291e-30    stage     II     264          184                *
#> 6: 5.198291e-30    stage    III     240          194              ***
#> 7: 5.198291e-30    stage     IV     132          117              ***
#>    sig_binary
#>        <lgcl>
#> 1:       TRUE
#> 2:      FALSE
#> 3:       TRUE
#> 4:      FALSE
#> 5:       TRUE
#> 6:       TRUE
#> 7:       TRUE

# Example 4: Exclude intercept for cleaner tables
clean_result <- m2dt(clintrial, glm_model, include_intercept = FALSE)
clean_result
#>      model_scope model_type             term     n events coefficient
#>           <char>     <char>           <char> <int>  <num>       <num>
#> 1: Multivariable   Logistic              age   850    628  0.04113579
#> 2: Multivariable   Logistic        sexFemale   450    324  0.00000000
#> 3: Multivariable   Logistic          sexMale   400    304  0.18742900
#> 4: Multivariable   Logistic treatmentControl   196    154  0.00000000
#> 5: Multivariable   Logistic  treatmentDrug A   292    183 -0.83712583
#> 6: Multivariable   Logistic  treatmentDrug B   362    291  0.06163894
#>            se        coef  coef_lower  coef_upper  exp_coef exp_lower exp_upper
#>         <num>       <num>       <num>       <num>     <num>     <num>     <num>
#> 1: 0.00712909  0.04113579  0.02716303  0.05510855 1.0419936 1.0275353 1.0566553
#> 2:         NA  0.00000000          NA          NA 1.0000000        NA        NA
#> 3: 0.16371242  0.18742900 -0.13344145  0.50829945 1.2061446 0.8750787 1.6624617
#> 4:         NA  0.00000000          NA          NA 1.0000000        NA        NA
#> 5: 0.21756836 -0.83712583 -1.26355198 -0.41069969 0.4329531 0.2826483 0.6631861
#> 6: 0.22320295  0.06163894 -0.37583081  0.49910869 1.0635783 0.6867185 1.6472524
#>           OR  ci_lower  ci_upper  statistic      p_value      AIC      BIC
#>        <num>     <num>     <num>      <num>        <num>    <num>    <num>
#> 1: 1.0419936 1.0275353 1.0566553  5.7701312 7.920986e-09 921.2366 944.9628
#> 2: 1.0000000        NA        NA         NA           NA 921.2366 944.9628
#> 3: 1.2061446 0.8750787 1.6624617  1.1448673 2.522641e-01 921.2366 944.9628
#> 4: 1.0000000        NA        NA         NA           NA 921.2366 944.9628
#> 5: 0.4329531 0.2826483 0.6631861 -3.8476451 1.192586e-04 921.2366 944.9628
#> 6: 1.0635783 0.6867185 1.6472524  0.2761565 7.824279e-01 921.2366 944.9628
#>    deviance null_deviance df_residual c_statistic hoslem_chi2   hoslem_p
#>       <num>         <num>       <int>       <num>       <num>      <num>
#> 1: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 2: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 3: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 4: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 5: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 6: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#>     variable   group n_group events_group reference    sig sig_binary
#>       <char>  <char>   <num>        <num>    <char> <char>     <lgcl>
#> 1:       age              NA           NA              ***       TRUE
#> 2:       sex  Female     450          324 reference             FALSE
#> 3:       sex    Male     400          304                       FALSE
#> 4: treatment Control     196          154 reference             FALSE
#> 5: treatment  Drug A     292          183              ***       TRUE
#> 6: treatment  Drug B     362          291                       FALSE

# Example 5: Change confidence level
result_90ci <- m2dt(clintrial, glm_model, conf_level = 0.90)
result_90ci
#>      model_scope model_type             term     n events coefficient
#>           <char>     <char>           <char> <int>  <num>       <num>
#> 1: Multivariable   Logistic      (Intercept)   850    628 -1.15943597
#> 2: Multivariable   Logistic              age   850    628  0.04113579
#> 3: Multivariable   Logistic        sexFemale   450    324  0.00000000
#> 4: Multivariable   Logistic          sexMale   400    304  0.18742900
#> 5: Multivariable   Logistic treatmentControl   196    154  0.00000000
#> 6: Multivariable   Logistic  treatmentDrug A   292    183 -0.83712583
#> 7: Multivariable   Logistic  treatmentDrug B   362    291  0.06163894
#>            se        coef  coef_lower coef_upper  exp_coef exp_lower exp_upper
#>         <num>       <num>       <num>      <num>     <num>     <num>     <num>
#> 1: 0.44641426 -1.15943597 -1.89372207 -0.4251499 0.3136630 0.1505106 0.6536718
#> 2: 0.00712909  0.04113579  0.02940948  0.0528621 1.0419936 1.0298462 1.0542842
#> 3:         NA  0.00000000          NA         NA 1.0000000        NA        NA
#> 4: 0.16371242  0.18742900 -0.08185397  0.4567120 1.2061446 0.9214065 1.5788741
#> 5:         NA  0.00000000          NA         NA 1.0000000        NA        NA
#> 6: 0.21756836 -0.83712583 -1.19499394 -0.4792577 0.4329531 0.3027058 0.6192429
#> 7: 0.22320295  0.06163894 -0.30549725  0.4287751 1.0635783 0.7367569 1.5353757
#>           OR  ci_lower  ci_upper  statistic      p_value      AIC      BIC
#>        <num>     <num>     <num>      <num>        <num>    <num>    <num>
#> 1: 0.3136630 0.1505106 0.6536718 -2.5972198 9.398175e-03 921.2366 944.9628
#> 2: 1.0419936 1.0298462 1.0542842  5.7701312 7.920986e-09 921.2366 944.9628
#> 3: 1.0000000        NA        NA         NA           NA 921.2366 944.9628
#> 4: 1.2061446 0.9214065 1.5788741  1.1448673 2.522641e-01 921.2366 944.9628
#> 5: 1.0000000        NA        NA         NA           NA 921.2366 944.9628
#> 6: 0.4329531 0.3027058 0.6192429 -3.8476451 1.192586e-04 921.2366 944.9628
#> 7: 1.0635783 0.7367569 1.5353757  0.2761565 7.824279e-01 921.2366 944.9628
#>    deviance null_deviance df_residual c_statistic hoslem_chi2   hoslem_p
#>       <num>         <num>       <int>       <num>       <num>      <num>
#> 1: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 2: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 3: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 4: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 5: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 6: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
#> 7: 911.2366      976.2826         845   0.6755824    13.77077 0.08793943
